directions.name: Restore accepted base, then test mirrored hot-band column sweep
directions.hypothesis: The 25.995744 ms regression came from the row-pair-dependent split column sweep. Restore the accepted grouped_rows=8 + reversed row-pair traversal + one-sync handoff baseline, then try a cleaner PTX hot-band variant that uses the mirrored column sweep uniformly for both row-pairs instead of branching the sweep by row-pair.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Bad instruction-flow and locality interaction from the failed row-pair-dependent column split, rather than the accepted base traversal itself.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu: hot-band column-order / PTX sweep logic
directions.risk: Moderate: the mirrored sweep may recover performance only if the accepted base is fully restored first; keep the row-pair-dependent split branch closed.
directions.stop_condition: If the mirrored sweep does not beat the measured regression while preserving the accepted base behavior, close this branch and do not reopen the row-pair-dependent split.

directions.name: Recover overlap behind the one-sync handoff
directions.hypothesis: Once the accepted grouped_rows=8 baseline is restored, there may still be slack after the one-sync handoff that can be recovered by carefully overlapping producer/consumer work without reopening the failed split-sweep logic.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Residual latency in the handoff window between row-pair traversal and the next shared-memory/compute phase.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu: shared-memory staging around the handoff
directions.risk: Higher complexity and easier to regress correctness than dir_01; keep the change local and avoid broad pipeline rewrites.
directions.stop_condition: If overlap changes force additional syncs or weaken the accepted traversal order, revert and stop.

directions.name: Small locality closure only
directions.hypothesis: A narrower locality-only cleanup may help if the current accepted base already exposes the main ceiling, but grouped_rows=16 should remain explicitly closed after the regression history.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Minor cache/shared-memory locality losses rather than a structural scheduling problem.
directions.risk: Low upside if the dominant issue is still the traversal/sweep shape; grouped_rows=16 should stay closed.
directions.stop_condition: Do not expand beyond locality-only edits; if grouped_rows=16 is needed, treat that as a rejected branch for this round.

notes: Anchored to latest measured run 20260420_085640_bf16_gemm_v1_f71a41f at 25.995744 ms. Use the accepted grouped_rows=8 + reversed row-pair traversal + one-sync handoff base as the starting point and keep rejected branches closed.
